LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, CAROL MOSS AND STEVE DOWNS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO THE 2 DEFENDANTS CAROL MOSS AND STEVE DOWN AND DENIED AS TO THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO THE REGENTS. GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANTS. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT'S REASONS FOR THE TERMINATION WERE PRETEXTUAL. GOVERNMENT CODE 818 PRECLUDES THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-05-2012 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-11-2012 Continued To Oct-29-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-11-2012 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-28-2012 Continued To May-14-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES -ATTY. BRIAN D. SUDANO, TEL. NO. (925) 849-4183 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LINDA MOIN MORONEY OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSED THAT THE GENERAL COUNSEL OF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA IS HIS GOOD FRIEND AND HE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR HIS RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, CAROL MOSS AND STEVE DOWNS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The motion for summary judgment is Granted as to the 2 defendants Carol Moss and Steve Down and denied as to the Regents of the University of California. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the 1st cause of action as to the Regents. Granted as to the request for punitive damages in favor of the defendants. There is a triable issue whether defendant's reasons for the termination were pretextual. Government Code 818 precludes the imposition of punitive damages. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =302/HK

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-25-2011 Set For Trial On Apr-23-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-06-12 At 03:00 P.M. In Dept 611
